title: Home
desc:
## Purpose

The Home screen is the place you begin every day.

Hermes intentionally avoids asking you to create a new to-do list every morning. Instead, it presents today's knowledge through **Today's Pursuit**, helping you begin with intention rather than deciding from scratch what to learn.

Everything shown on this page exists to answer one question:

> "What deserves my attention today?"

---

## Today's Pursuit

Today's Pursuit contains the knowledge scheduled specifically for the current day.

Items may be added manually, or automatically generated from your Manual Collections according to the Daily Limits you configured.

Today's Pursuit can contain:

- Questions
- Articles
- Notes
- Ideas
- Observations

Each item is intentionally limited so your day never becomes overwhelming.

Rather than showing everything you have collected, Hermes slowly surfaces knowledge over time.

---

## Starter Workspace

Every new installation includes a Starter Workspace.

The Starter Workspace exists to teach Hermes by example.

Inside it you'll discover every supported Item type together with their available actions and settings.

Nothing inside the Starter Workspace is permanent.

Once you understand the system, you are encouraged to create your own Workspace and remove the Starter Workspace entirely.

---

## Pinned Domains

Pinned Domains provide quick access to the areas of your life you visit most frequently.

Instead of searching every time, your most important Domains remain immediately available from the Home screen.

Examples:

• Engineering

• Mathematics

• Startup

• Philosophy

• Health

Pinning only changes visibility on the Home screen.

It does not affect the underlying organization.

---

## Pinned Blocks

Blocks represent focused subjects inside a Domain.

Pinning Blocks allows instant access to subjects you work on daily.

Example:

Engineering

↓

Python

↓

Pinned Block

This saves navigation while keeping the overall hierarchy clean.

---

## Recent Evolutios

Recent Evolutios display the latest pieces of knowledge that changed your understanding.

These are not simply recent notes.

An Evolutio represents a meaningful shift in your thinking.

Seeing them together provides a quick reminder of how your understanding is gradually evolving over time.

---

## Today's Veritas

Consistency is not always possible.

Sometimes life interrupts.

Veritas exists to record the truth rather than punish interruptions.

Whether today involved illness, travel, examinations, or rest, Hermes allows you to honestly document why your routine changed.

Knowledge grows best when reality is acknowledged rather than ignored.

---

## Workspace Menu

The current Workspace appears in the upper-right corner of the Home screen.

Selecting it opens Workspace Settings.

From here you can:

• View Archive

Browse archived items without mixing them into your active workspace.

• Visibility

Control which information appears on the Home screen.

• Lock Workspace

Protect the workspace using a PIN for private knowledge.

• Switch Workspace

Move between completely independent knowledge systems.

• Reload

Refresh the current workspace if changes were made elsewhere.

---

The Home screen is intentionally minimal.

Its purpose is not to show everything you own.

Its purpose is to quietly answer one question every morning:

"What deserves my attention today?"

title: Domains
desc:
## Purpose

Domains represent the highest level of organization inside Hermes.

A Domain is not a folder for random files. Instead, it represents a long-term area of your life that you continuously learn, build, or improve.

Examples include:

- Computer Science
- Mathematics
- Startup
- Philosophy
- Health
- Finance
- Personal Growth

Think of a Domain as a place where knowledge naturally belongs for years rather than days.

---

## Why Domains Exist

Without organization, every note eventually becomes difficult to find.

Hermes intentionally asks you to assign knowledge to a Domain before storing it. This small amount of friction keeps your knowledge base organized instead of becoming another collection of scattered notes.

Rather than asking:

> "Where did I save this?"

Hermes encourages you to think:

> "Which part of my life does this belong to?"

---

## Navigating Domains

Selecting a Domain opens its Blocks.

A Domain does not directly contain notes or questions.

Instead, it contains multiple Blocks that divide large subjects into smaller areas.

For example:

Computer Science

↓

Python

↓

Questions

↓

Articles

↓

Ideas

↓

Notes

This hierarchy keeps knowledge easy to navigate even after years of use.

---

## Pinning Domains

Frequently used Domains can be pinned.

Pinned Domains appear directly on the Home screen, allowing quick access without navigating through the entire hierarchy every day.

Pinning only changes visibility.

It does not move or duplicate your data.

---

## Domain Settings

Each Domain has its own options menu.

From here you can manage the Domain, rename it, adjust its appearance, or perform future management actions without affecting other Domains.

Domains are independent from one another.

Changing one Domain never changes another.

---

## Why This Design?

Many note-taking applications organize everything using folders.

Hermes instead organizes knowledge using Domains because learning is usually continuous rather than temporary.

Programming is not a folder.

Mathematics is not a folder.

Philosophy is not a folder.

They are lifelong disciplines.

Domains reflect that idea.

---

Once you choose a Domain, you enter its Blocks, where broad areas become focused subjects ready to hold your knowledge.

title: blocks
desc:
# Blocks

## Purpose

Blocks divide a large Domain into focused areas of study.

A Domain represents a broad, long-term discipline, while a Block represents a specific subject within that discipline.

For example:

Engineering

↓

Python

↓

Questions

Articles

Ideas

Notes

By introducing Blocks, Hermes keeps knowledge organized without forcing everything into one enormous collection.

---

## Why Blocks Exist

Learning rarely happens across an entire Domain at once.

You don't study all of Engineering in one session.

Instead, you focus on one subject at a time.

Python.

Probability.

Operating Systems.

Deep Learning.

Blocks exist to create these focused environments where every piece of related knowledge naturally belongs together.

Instead of asking:

> "Where should I save this?"

Hermes encourages:

> "Which subject does this belong to?"

This makes navigation simpler and keeps every Block meaningful even after years of learning.

---

## Entering a Block

Selecting a Block opens the Items inside it.

A Block becomes your working environment.

Here you'll find every type of knowledge related to that subject, including:

• Questions

• Articles

• Notes

• Ideas

• Observations

• Reflections

• Evolutios

Each Item contributes to your understanding from a different perspective.

---

## Pinning Blocks

Some Blocks become part of your everyday routine.

For example:

• Probability

• PyTorch

• Linux

• Startup

Rather than navigating through the hierarchy every day, frequently used Blocks can be pinned.

Pinned Blocks appear directly on the Home screen, allowing quick access while preserving the overall organization.

Pinning never duplicates or moves your knowledge.

It simply creates a shortcut.

---

## Block Settings

Each Block provides its own management options.

From here you can:

• Rename the Block

• Pin or unpin it

• Archive it when it is no longer active

• Manage future settings as Hermes evolves

Every Block remains independent.

Changes inside one Block never affect another.

---

## Long-Term Learning

Blocks are designed for continuous growth.

Unlike temporary folders that eventually become abandoned, a Block evolves alongside your understanding.

A Python Block may begin with basic syntax, gradually accumulate notes, articles, reflections, and questions, and eventually become a complete knowledge base built over months or even years.

Hermes treats Blocks as living collections rather than static folders.

---

## The Next Step

Once inside a Block, Hermes presents its Items.

Items are the smallest units of knowledge inside Hermes.

Each Item type serves a different purpose in helping you understand, reflect, and grow.

title: Items
desc:
## Purpose

Everything you store inside Hermes is an Item.

An Item is the smallest unit of knowledge within your workspace. Rather than forcing every piece of information into a single note format, Hermes recognizes that different kinds of thinking deserve different environments.

A question is not a note.

An observation is not an idea.

An article is not a reflection.

Each Item type exists for a different stage of learning.

---

## Why Different Item Types Exist

Most knowledge applications store everything as plain text.

Over time, every note begins to look the same.

Questions become mixed with ideas.

Research becomes mixed with observations.

Reflections become buried beneath copied articles.

Eventually, finding anything meaningful becomes difficult.

Hermes avoids this by giving every type of knowledge its own dedicated workflow.

Instead of asking:

> "Where should I write this?"

Hermes encourages:

> "What kind of knowledge is this?"

That simple decision keeps your workspace organized naturally.

---

## Item Types

Every Block can contain several different kinds of Items.

Each one serves a unique purpose.

### Questions

Questions encourage active learning.

Instead of passively consuming information, you attempt an answer, compare it with the official solution, reflect on your understanding, and gradually improve over time.

Questions are designed to strengthen understanding rather than memory.

---

### Articles

Articles provide a calm reading experience.

Hermes removes unnecessary distractions and allows you to focus entirely on the content.

After reading, you may simply mark the article as read or write a Reflection if the article changed your thinking.

Reading is not measured by completion.

It is measured by understanding.

---

### Notes

Notes capture knowledge in your own words.

Rather than copying information directly from an article, Notes represent your interpretation after understanding the topic.

They also support keywords and connections to related Items, allowing knowledge to naturally form a network instead of remaining isolated.

---

### Observations

Observations record small moments that are easy to forget.

A conversation.

A pattern.

A realization.

Something you noticed during everyday life.

Many observations seem insignificant when first written.

Months later, they often become the foundation of much larger ideas.

Observations can later be converted into Reflections or Ideas as they mature.

---

### Ideas

Ideas are possibilities.

Sometimes a single sentence grows into an entire project.

Hermes allows ideas to evolve naturally by expanding them over time, recording potential applications, connecting them with related knowledge, promoting them into Projects, or archiving them until the right moment arrives.

Ideas are never expected to be complete on the first day.

---

### Reflections

Reflection is where learning becomes understanding.

Reading something does not guarantee that it changed you.

Reflection asks one simple question:

> "What changed in the way you think?"

Reflections help transform information into personal understanding.

---

### Evolutios

An Evolutio represents a genuine cognitive shift.

Not every Reflection becomes an Evolutio.

Only those moments where your understanding fundamentally changes deserve this distinction.

Hermes considers Evolutios to be the true measure of growth.

---

## Navigation

Selecting any Item opens its dedicated workspace.

Each Item type provides tools specifically designed for that form of knowledge.

Questions focus on solving.

Articles focus on reading.

Ideas focus on expansion.

Observations focus on noticing.

Notes focus on understanding.

This allows every workflow to remain simple while still providing powerful features.

---

## Growing Your Knowledge

Over time, a single Block naturally becomes a rich collection of interconnected knowledge.

Questions lead to Notes.

Articles inspire Reflections.

Observations become Ideas.

Ideas evolve into Projects.

Reflections become Evolutios.

Hermes is designed so that knowledge does not remain isolated.

Instead, every Item contributes to a continuously evolving understanding.

---

The following sections explore each Item type individually, explaining their workflows, features, and the philosophy behind why they exist.

title: Questions
desc:
## Purpose

Questions are the foundation of deliberate learning inside Hermes.

Most learning applications encourage passive consumption. You read articles, watch videos, or highlight text and eventually move on.

Hermes takes a different approach.

Instead of beginning with answers, Hermes begins with questions.

A well-constructed question creates curiosity. It exposes gaps in your understanding and encourages you to think before seeking a solution.

Questions transform learning from passive reading into active reasoning.

---

## Why Questions Exist

Reading an explanation often creates the illusion of understanding.

Answering a question reveals whether that understanding actually exists.

Hermes intentionally presents Questions before explanations because the process of thinking is where learning truly happens.

Rather than asking you to remember information, Hermes encourages you to construct it yourself.

---

## Creating Questions

Questions can be created manually whenever curiosity arises.

You can also import hundreds or even thousands of questions through Manual Collections using structured JSON files.

This allows Hermes to gradually schedule meaningful questions over days, weeks, or months instead of overwhelming you all at once.

Questions may come from:

- Your own curiosity
- AI-generated question sets
- Books
- Courses
- Interview preparation
- Competitive programming
- Mathematics practice
- Research topics

Hermes treats every question equally.

---

## Today's Pursuit

Questions imported into Manual Collections do not immediately appear.

Instead, Hermes schedules them into Today's Pursuit according to the Daily Limit configured for that collection.

For example:

Daily Limit: 2 Questions

Today:

Question 1

Question 2

Tomorrow:

Question 3

Question 4

This creates a sustainable learning rhythm rather than an endless backlog.

---

## Solving a Question

Each Question follows the same deliberate workflow.

Question

↓

Think independently

↓

Write your answer

↓

Reveal the official answer

↓

Reflect (Optional)

↓

Complete

Every stage has a purpose.

Hermes intentionally delays showing the official answer until after you have genuinely attempted the problem.

---

## Your Answer

Your answer represents your current understanding.

It is never compared for correctness automatically.

Instead, Hermes preserves it as a snapshot of how you thought at that moment.

Over time, this becomes an incredibly valuable record of your learning journey.

Even after completion, your answer remains editable.

Understanding evolves, and your explanations should be free to evolve as well.

---

## Official Solution

Once you reveal the solution, Hermes presents the original answer alongside your own.

The goal is not to score yourself.

The goal is to compare reasoning.

Sometimes your answer may be different yet equally valid.

Sometimes a single missing concept becomes obvious immediately.

Learning happens through comparison rather than memorization.

---

## Reflection

Reflection is optional.

Not every question changes your thinking.

However, when it does, Hermes encourages you to record what changed.

Examples include:

"I misunderstood time complexity."

"I finally understood why recursion works."

"I kept confusing processes with threads."

Reflections capture understanding rather than correctness.

Like your answer, reflections remain editable even after completion.

---

## Completing a Question

Completing a question records your progress.

Hermes preserves:

- Your answer
- The official solution
- Your reflection
- Completion date

Completed questions remain available for future review.

Learning should never disappear after pressing a button.

---

## Reviewing Completed Questions

At any time, you can reopen a completed question.

Hermes displays:

- The original question
- Your answer
- The official solution
- Your reflection
- Completion date

This allows you to revisit your earlier thinking and observe how your understanding has evolved.

---

## Skip for Today

Sometimes today simply is not the right day.

Instead of forcing yourself through every question, Hermes allows you to skip it.

Skipping:

- Removes the question from Today's Pursuit
- Immediately schedules another eligible question
- Does not mark the question as completed
- Allows it to return on a future day

This keeps your momentum without losing valuable questions.

---

## Why This Workflow?

Most learning software measures completion.

Hermes measures engagement.

A completed checkbox says very little.

A written answer, thoughtful comparison, and personal reflection reveal genuine understanding.

Questions exist to challenge your thinking, not simply to test your memory.

Every completed question becomes another step toward deeper understanding rather than another item crossed off a list.

title: Articles
desc:
## Purpose

Articles are where Hermes slows everything down.

The modern internet is designed to maximize attention rather than understanding. Pop-ups, advertisements, notifications, autoplay videos, and endless recommendations constantly interrupt the reading experience.

Hermes removes that noise.

An Article exists to create a quiet environment where your attention belongs entirely to the knowledge in front of you.

Reading should feel calm, intentional, and uninterrupted.

---

## Why Articles Exist

Saving hundreds of bookmarks does not mean you will ever read them.

Reading an article once does not mean you understood it.

Hermes treats Articles as part of a long-term learning journey rather than temporary browser tabs.

Instead of asking:

> "How many articles have I collected?"

Hermes encourages a different question:

> "How many ideas have I truly understood?"

That small shift changes reading from collecting information into building understanding.

---

## Reading Inside Hermes

Articles are rendered locally inside Hermes.

Instead of opening the original webpage, Hermes presents the content in a distraction-free reader designed specifically for learning.

The reader supports:

- Clean typography
- Markdown rendering
- LaTeX mathematics
- Code blocks
- Comfortable spacing
- Offline reading

The goal is simple.

Once reading begins, the interface should disappear.

Only the knowledge should remain.

---

## Mark as Read

Completing an article does not remove it from your knowledge base.

Instead, Hermes records that you have read it.

The article remains available for future reference whenever you need it again.

Inside Block lists, previously read articles are displayed using a softer visual appearance, allowing you to immediately distinguish between:

- New articles
- Previously surfaced articles
- Articles you have already completed

This becomes especially valuable when your library grows into hundreds or even thousands of articles.

---

## Reflection

Reading is only the first step.

Sometimes an article simply provides information.

Other times, it changes how you think.

Whenever that happens, Hermes encourages you to write a Reflection.

A Reflection answers a simple question:

> "What changed in my understanding after reading this?"

Reflections are entirely optional.

Not every article deserves one.

But when an article genuinely changes your perspective, recording that realization allows your future self to revisit not only the article, but also the way it shaped your thinking.

---

## Articles in Today's Pursuit

Articles can appear automatically inside Today's Pursuit when they are imported through Manual Collections.

Rather than asking you to decide what to read every morning, Hermes gradually surfaces articles according to the Daily Limit configured for each collection.

For example:

Reading Collection

Daily Limit: 1

Today:

One carefully selected article.

Tomorrow:

Another article.

This keeps reading sustainable instead of overwhelming.

---

## Long-Term Reading

Hermes is designed for readers who continuously collect knowledge.

Instead of maintaining hundreds of forgotten browser bookmarks, Articles become part of an organized knowledge system.

You can always return to an article, reread it, update your Reflection, or connect it with other knowledge inside your workspace.

Nothing is lost.

Nothing becomes buried.

Everything remains discoverable.

---

## Why This Workflow?

Most reading applications measure completion.

Hermes measures understanding.

Finishing an article is useful.

Remembering how that article changed your thinking is far more valuable.

Articles are not simply things to finish.

They are opportunities to deepen your understanding, one thoughtful reading session at a time.

title: Notes
desc:
## Purpose

Notes preserve understanding in your own words.

Most note-taking applications become digital storage rooms filled with copied text, screenshots, and links. Months later, it becomes difficult to remember which notes mattered and which were simply copied for "later."

Hermes encourages a different philosophy.

A Note should represent what *you understand*, not what someone else wrote.

Instead of becoming another archive of information, Notes become an archive of your thinking.

---

## Why Notes Exist

Understanding becomes stronger when ideas are rewritten in your own words.

After reading an article, solving a problem, or learning a new concept, there is often a moment where everything suddenly becomes clear.

That understanding deserves its own place.

Notes capture those moments before they disappear.

Rather than asking:

> "What did this article say?"

Hermes encourages:

> "How would I explain this to someone else?"

That simple shift transforms information into knowledge.

---

## Writing Notes

Notes use plain Markdown.

Markdown was intentionally chosen because it is simple, readable, portable, and future-proof.

Your knowledge should never depend on proprietary file formats or cloud services.

Years from now, your Notes should remain just as readable as the day you wrote them.

Hermes stores your knowledge in a format that belongs to you.

---

## Connecting Notes

Knowledge rarely exists in isolation.

While writing a Note, you can connect it to other Items across your workspace.

For example:

Operating Systems

↓

Process Scheduling

↓

Connected Note

↓

CPU Context Switching

These connections create a growing network of understanding instead of isolated documents.

As your workspace expands, related ideas naturally become easier to discover.

---

## Keywords

Each Note can include custom keywords.

Keywords improve discoverability without changing the structure of your workspace.

For example, a Note about SQLite might include:

- Database
- SQL
- B-Tree
- Storage Engine
- Indexing

Later, searching any of these terms immediately surfaces the Note.

Keywords complement your organization rather than replacing it.

---

## Editing Notes

Understanding changes over time.

Hermes encourages continuous refinement rather than permanent drafts.

Every Note remains editable.

As you gain new insights, you can revisit previous Notes, clarify explanations, expand ideas, and improve your understanding without creating unnecessary duplicates.

A Note grows alongside you.

---

## Notes as Building Blocks

Many larger ideas begin as small Notes.

A single Note may eventually inspire:

- An Observation
- A Reflection
- An Idea
- A Project
- An Evolutio

Nothing inside Hermes exists in isolation.

Knowledge naturally grows through connections.

---

## Long-Term Value

Over months and years, your Notes become more than documentation.

They become a personal textbook written entirely by you.

Instead of searching the internet for the same explanations repeatedly, you gradually build a collection of concepts explained in the way that makes the most sense to you.

No one understands your learning style better than your past self.

---

## Why This Workflow?

Copying information is effortless.

Explaining it in your own words requires understanding.

Hermes values the second far more than the first.

Notes exist to preserve your understanding, allowing your future self to revisit not just what you learned, but how you understood it.

title: Observations
desc:
## Purpose

Not every meaningful thought begins as a complete idea.

Sometimes it is a sentence you overhear.

A pattern you notice.

A mistake you keep repeating.

A small realization during a lecture.

A conversation with a friend.

Most of these moments disappear within hours because they never get recorded.

Observations exist to preserve those moments before they are forgotten.

---

## Why Observations Exist

Many of our most valuable insights don't arrive fully formed.

They begin as something subtle.

Perhaps you notice that you understand mathematics better after solving problems than after watching videos.

Perhaps you realize that every time you procrastinate, you first open social media.

Perhaps you observe that reading slowly helps you retain more than reading quickly.

These aren't Ideas yet.

They aren't Reflections yet.

They're simply things worth remembering.

Hermes gives those moments a home.

---

## Recording an Observation

An Observation should be quick.

It is not expected to be polished.

It doesn't need paragraphs of explanation.

Simply capture what you noticed while it is still fresh.

The goal is preservation, not perfection.

---

## Why Was It Worth Recording?

Every Observation contains an optional section:

**Why was this worth recording?**

This small prompt encourages intentional thinking.

Instead of merely storing an observation, you pause for a moment and explain why it mattered.

Sometimes the answer is simple.

"I keep making this mistake."

Sometimes it's deeper.

"This changes how I think about learning."

This habit transforms random notes into meaningful knowledge.

---

## Connect Related Items

Observations rarely exist alone.

You can connect an Observation with:

- Notes
- Questions
- Articles
- Ideas
- Reflections
- Evolutios

Over time, these connections reveal relationships that may not have been obvious when the Observation was first recorded.

Knowledge becomes a network instead of isolated documents.

---

## Recognizing Patterns

One Observation is interesting.

Ten similar Observations reveal a pattern.

As your workspace grows, you may begin to notice recurring themes.

Perhaps every Observation about your productivity points toward the same problem.

Perhaps every programming Observation leads back to one missing concept.

Hermes encourages pattern recognition because understanding often emerges from repetition rather than isolated events.

---

## Convert to Reflection

Sometimes an Observation changes the way you think.

When that happens, it no longer belongs as a simple Observation.

It can be promoted into a Reflection.

This allows your workspace to naturally evolve as your understanding becomes clearer.

Nothing is duplicated.

The knowledge simply grows into its next stage.

---

## Convert to Idea

Not every Observation is about understanding.

Some become opportunities.

A recurring frustration.

A missing feature.

A business possibility.

A research direction.

When an Observation begins suggesting action rather than explanation, it can be converted into an Idea.

This preserves the original thought while allowing it to grow into something larger.

---

## Long-Term Value

Observations often become the seeds of your most meaningful work.

Months later, you may discover that an Observation you almost ignored became:

- a project,
- a startup,
- a research topic,
- or a completely new way of thinking.

The smallest observations frequently lead to the biggest changes.

Hermes exists to ensure those moments are never lost simply because they seemed too small to matter.

---

## Why This Workflow?

The human mind notices far more than it remembers.

Observations exist because memory fades quickly.

Recording what you notice today allows your future self to discover patterns that would otherwise remain invisible.

Every significant insight begins with someone noticing something that others overlooked.

title: Ideas
desc:
## Purpose

Ideas are possibilities waiting to grow.

Not every thought arrives fully formed. Some begin as a single sentence, a moment of curiosity, or a problem that refuses to leave your mind.

Hermes treats Ideas as living pieces of knowledge rather than temporary notes.

Instead of expecting an idea to be complete on the first day, Hermes provides an environment where it can mature naturally over time.

---

## Why Ideas Exist

Many good ideas disappear because they are captured too late.

Others disappear because they are written down once and never revisited.

Hermes was designed to solve both problems.

An Idea is not expected to be perfect.

It only needs to exist.

Once recorded, it can gradually evolve through expansion, experimentation, connections, and reflection.

Great projects rarely begin as detailed plans.

Most begin as incomplete thoughts.

---

## Recording an Idea

Creating an Idea should be effortless.

Give it a short title that captures the essence of the thought.

Examples:

- Local AI Knowledge System
- Better FFmpeg Timeline
- Mathematical Visualizer
- Linux Workflow Improvements

The title is only the beginning.

Everything else can evolve later.

---

## Expand Idea

Ideas rarely stop growing after their first sentence.

The **Expand Idea** section allows you to continuously develop the original thought.

Add:

- new discoveries
- design decisions
- technical approaches
- challenges
- future improvements

Unlike rewriting the title, expansion preserves the original idea while documenting how it grows over time.

Months later, you'll be able to see exactly how a small thought evolved into something much larger.

---

## Potential Applications

Every idea has multiple possible directions.

Instead of mixing them into the main description, Hermes provides a dedicated section for Potential Applications.

Here you can explore:

- where the idea could be useful
- who might benefit from it
- possible products
- research opportunities
- startup possibilities

Separating applications from the core idea keeps both sections focused while encouraging broader thinking.

---

## Connect Related Items

Ideas rarely exist in isolation.

An Idea can be connected with other knowledge inside Hermes.

For example:

Idea

↓

Related Observation

↓

Supporting Article

↓

Relevant Note

↓

Solved Question

↓

Reflection

These connections allow ideas to grow using knowledge you've already collected.

Instead of searching through dozens of notes, everything related to an idea remains only a few clicks away.

Knowledge becomes a connected network rather than isolated documents.

---

## Promote to Project

Some ideas eventually become real work.

When an Idea reaches that stage, it can be promoted into a Project.

Promoted Ideas receive special visual emphasis, making them easy to identify among unfinished thoughts.

Promotion does not remove the idea.

It simply acknowledges that it has moved from possibility into execution.

If priorities change later, the Project status can be removed without losing any information.

---

## Archive

Not every idea needs immediate attention.

Some ideas are simply waiting for the right moment.

Archiving an Idea removes it from your active workspace while preserving everything attached to it.

Nothing is deleted.

Whenever the time feels right, the Idea can be restored exactly as it was.

Ideas deserve patience just as much as action.

---

## Revisiting Ideas

One of the most valuable habits inside Hermes is returning to old ideas.

A concept that seemed impossible six months ago may suddenly become achievable because your knowledge has grown.

Hermes encourages revisiting unfinished thoughts rather than constantly chasing new ones.

Progress often comes from developing existing ideas rather than replacing them.

---

## Long-Term Value

Over time, your Ideas become a record of your curiosity.

Some remain simple thoughts.

Some become personal experiments.

Some become research.

Some become startups.

Some quietly inspire completely different projects years later.

Hermes exists to preserve every stage of that journey.

---

## Why This Workflow?

Ideas are fragile.

Most disappear because they were never given room to grow.

Hermes provides that room.

By allowing ideas to expand, connect, evolve, and eventually become projects, Hermes transforms fleeting inspiration into something that can genuinely shape your future.

The goal is not to collect ideas.

The goal is to cultivate them.

title: Evolutio
desc:
## Purpose

An Evolutio is the highest form of knowledge inside Hermes.

Not every Note becomes an Evolutio.

Not every Reflection becomes an Evolutio.

An Evolutio represents a genuine shift in the way you think.

It is the moment where understanding permanently changes.

Hermes considers these moments to be the true measure of growth.

---

## Why Evolutios Exist

Most productivity applications measure activity.

Tasks completed.

Pages read.

Hours studied.

Streaks maintained.

Hermes measures something different.

It measures moments where your understanding fundamentally changed.

Reading ten articles means very little if your thinking remains the same.

One powerful realization can change the direction of your learning forever.

Hermes exists to preserve those moments.

---

## What Makes an Evolutio?

An Evolutio is not created because something was completed.

It is created because something changed.

Examples include:

"I finally understood recursion."

"I realized consistency matters more than motivation."

"I discovered that understanding comes from solving problems, not watching videos."

"I stopped treating Linux as an operating system and started seeing it as a collection of small tools."

These are not facts.

They are transformations.

---

## Promoting a Reflection

Most Evolutios begin as Reflections.

While writing a Reflection, you may realize:

"This changed the way I think."

When that happens, the Reflection can be promoted into an Evolutio.

Nothing is duplicated.

The knowledge simply moves into its highest form.

This ensures Evolutios remain meaningful instead of becoming another collection of ordinary notes.

---

## Why Evolutios Matter

Memory fades.

Understanding evolves.

Without recording these moments, it becomes surprisingly difficult to remember how your thinking developed.

Months later, you may remember learning recursion.

You probably won't remember the exact realization that finally made it click.

Hermes preserves that moment forever.

---

## Recent Evolutios

The Home screen displays your recent Evolutios.

This is intentional.

Hermes wants you to see your growth rather than your productivity.

Looking back at recent Evolutios reminds you that your understanding continues to evolve, even during periods that feel slow.

Growth is often easier to recognize in hindsight.

---

## Evolution Timeline

Every Evolutio becomes part of your personal Evolution timeline.

Rather than showing completed tasks, the timeline records meaningful moments where your perspective changed.

Over months and years, it becomes a visual history of your intellectual development.

Instead of asking:

"How much did I do?"

Hermes encourages:

"How much have I changed?"

---

## Connected Knowledge

Each Evolutio remains connected to the journey that created it.

You can revisit:

- The original Question
- The Article you read
- Your Notes
- Observations
- Ideas
- Reflection

Every Evolutio preserves not only the destination, but also the path that led there.

---

## Looking Back

One of the most rewarding experiences inside Hermes is reading an Evolutio from months or years ago.

Sometimes you smile because something that once felt impossible now feels obvious.

Sometimes you discover that an old realization still shapes the way you work today.

Your Evolutios become conversations between different versions of yourself.

---

## Long-Term Value

A collection of completed tasks tells you what you did.

A collection of Evolutios tells you who you became.

That difference defines Hermes.

The goal was never to help you finish more work.

The goal was to preserve the moments that permanently changed the way you think.

---

## Why This Workflow?

Knowledge is temporary.

Understanding can last a lifetime.

Hermes considers every Evolutio to be proof that learning has become transformation.

They are not achievements.

They are milestones in the continuous evolution of your mind.

title: Veritas
desc:
## Purpose

Growth is rarely a straight line.

Some days you study for hours.

Some days life has other plans.

Traditional productivity applications often treat these interruptions as failures. Miss a day, and your streak disappears. Your progress resets. The software quietly tells you that you have fallen behind.

Hermes rejects that philosophy.

Instead of recording failure, Hermes records truth.

That truth is called **Veritas**.

---

## Why Veritas Exists

Consistency is important.

Perfection is impossible.

Everyone experiences days when learning simply cannot be the highest priority.

Examinations.

Family.

Travel.

Illness.

Rest.

Unexpected responsibilities.

These moments should not erase months of genuine progress.

Veritas exists because reality deserves to be recorded honestly.

---

## What Is a Veritas?

A Veritas is a short record explaining why your normal learning routine paused.

It is not an excuse.

It is not an apology.

It is simply the truth.

Examples include:

- Today was dedicated to semester examinations.
- I travelled with my family.
- I wasn't feeling physically well.
- I needed rest after several intense days of work.
- Unexpected responsibilities took priority today.

Nothing more is required.

---

## Why Not Streaks?

Hermes intentionally avoids traditional streak systems.

A streak only measures uninterrupted activity.

It says nothing about understanding.

Imagine studying consistently for one hundred days.

On the one hundred and first day you become ill.

Many applications reset your progress back to zero.

Nothing about your understanding disappeared.

Nothing about your previous effort became meaningless.

Only the calendar changed.

Hermes refuses to confuse absence with failure.

---

## Daily Veritas

Every day includes the opportunity to record a Veritas.

If your learning proceeded normally, you simply continue.

If life interrupted your plans, you can preserve that reality instead of pretending it never happened.

This creates an honest timeline of your personal growth.

---

## Adding Veritas

Creating a Veritas takes only a moment.

Choose the day.

Describe what happened.

Save.

That day now becomes part of your journey instead of becoming an unexplained gap.

Sometimes a single sentence is enough.

Sometimes you may choose to write more.

Both are equally valid.

---

## Veritas Timeline

Veritas entries appear alongside your Evolutios.

This is intentional.

Growth is not only measured by breakthroughs.

It is also shaped by interruptions, recovery, patience, and persistence.

Seeing both together creates a far more truthful picture of your life than an unbroken chain of completed tasks ever could.

---

## Looking Back

Months later, your Veritas entries often tell stories you would otherwise forget.

You may remember:

- the semester that demanded all of your attention,
- the week you travelled,
- the period you recovered from burnout,
- the days you deliberately rested before returning stronger.

Those moments are part of your journey.

Ignoring them would make the story incomplete.

---

## Compassion Through Honesty

Hermes does not reward guilt.

It does not punish rest.

It simply asks for honesty.

The goal is not to justify your absence.

The goal is to acknowledge reality exactly as it happened.

Sometimes the healthiest decision is to stop working.

Hermes considers that just as important to record as a productive day.

---

## Long-Term Value

Years from now, your Veritas entries become more than explanations.

They become reminders that growth was never perfect.

There were pauses.

There were setbacks.

There were moments when life demanded something else.

Yet your journey continued.

That is what matters.

---

## Why This Workflow?

Growth is measured over years, not days.

Missing one day does not erase months of learning.

Recording the truth is more valuable than preserving an artificial streak.

Hermes believes that honesty builds stronger habits than guilt ever will.

Veritas exists to protect that philosophy.

Because real growth includes both progress and pause.

title: Search
desc:
## Purpose

Knowledge only becomes valuable when it can be rediscovered.

Most people remember learning something.

Very few remember exactly where they saved it.

Was it inside a folder?

A notebook?

A bookmark?

A browser tab?

A PDF?

Months later, finding that information often becomes harder than learning it again.

Hermes was designed to solve that problem.

Search allows you to find ideas rather than files.

---

## Why Search Exists

Human memory is associative.

You rarely remember where you stored something.

Instead, you remember fragments.

A keyword.

A concept.

A sentence.

A person's name.

A technology.

Hermes embraces this natural way of remembering.

Instead of forcing you to remember locations, Hermes helps you rediscover knowledge through meaning.

---

## Search Everything

Hermes searches across your entire workspace.

Results may include:

- Questions
- Articles
- Notes
- Observations
- Ideas
- Reflections
- Evolutios

You don't need to remember which Block contains the information.

Hermes searches the content itself.

---

## More Than File Names

Traditional search often depends on filenames.

Hermes searches much deeper.

It looks through:

- Titles
- Content
- Keywords
- Markdown
- Connected Items

This allows ideas to become discoverable even if you forgot exactly where they were written.

---

## Connected Knowledge

Because Items inside Hermes can be connected together, Search often reveals more than a single result.

For example:

Searching:

"SQLite"

may return:

- A Question
- An Article
- Several Notes
- An Observation
- A Reflection
- An Evolutio

Instead of isolated files, Hermes presents an entire network of related understanding.

---

## Local First

Search is completely local.

Nothing leaves your device.

There are no cloud servers indexing your knowledge.

Every search happens inside your own workspace.

This keeps searching fast while preserving complete ownership of your information.

---

## Growing With You

As your knowledge base expands, Search becomes increasingly valuable.

Hundreds of Notes.

Thousands of Questions.

Years of Articles.

Without an effective search system, this knowledge eventually becomes difficult to navigate.

Hermes ensures that growth never becomes clutter.

The larger your workspace becomes, the more important Search becomes.

---

## A Personal Knowledge Engine

Search is more than navigation.

It becomes a way of rediscovering ideas you had forgotten.

Many users will search for one topic and unexpectedly reconnect with observations, reflections, or ideas they recorded months earlier.

This creates new opportunities for learning and connection.

Sometimes the most valuable discovery is not something new.

It is something you already knew but had forgotten.

---

## Why This Workflow?

Knowledge should never disappear simply because time has passed.

Hermes believes that remembering ideas should feel effortless.

You should only need to remember **what** you were thinking about.

Hermes takes care of remembering **where** you wrote it.

Search transforms your workspace from a collection of files into a living library of your own understanding.

title: Manual Collections
desc:
## Purpose

Knowledge should never enter your mind by accident.

Modern applications constantly push information toward you.

Recommended videos.

Suggested articles.

Trending posts.

Infinite feeds.

Hermes intentionally moves in the opposite direction.

Instead of pushing knowledge toward you, Hermes allows you to deliberately bring knowledge into your own system.

Manual Collections are the beginning of that process.

They are the place where your long-term knowledge journey starts.

---

## Why Manual Collections Exist

Imagine finding one hundred excellent articles about Deep Learning.

Reading all of them in a single week is unrealistic.

Even if you tried, very little would be retained.

Instead of treating knowledge as something to consume immediately, Hermes allows you to build a personal knowledge pipeline.

You collect knowledge once.

Hermes gradually introduces it into your daily learning through Today's Pursuit.

This transforms overwhelming collections into sustainable daily progress.

---

## What Is a Manual Collection?

A Manual Collection is a local container that stores knowledge you intentionally imported.

Each collection belongs to a specific Domain and Block.

For example:

Engineering

↓

Deep Learning

↓

Manual Collection

Inside a collection you may store:

- Questions
- Articles

Each collection remains completely local and fully under your control.

---

## Importing Knowledge

Hermes currently supports importing structured JSON files.

Rather than manually creating hundreds of Questions or Articles one by one, you can prepare them once and import them in seconds.

This makes it practical to build large knowledge collections that continue serving you for months.

For example:

• 500 Probability Questions

• 1,000 Linux Articles

• 300 PyTorch Exercises

• Research Papers

Everything becomes part of your personal learning system.

---

## Daily Scheduling

Importing knowledge does not immediately flood your workspace.

Instead, every collection has a configurable Daily Limit.

For example:

Probability Questions

Daily Limit:

2 Questions

Every day Hermes selects two unanswered questions from the collection and places them into Today's Pursuit.

Tomorrow another two appear.

The collection slowly unfolds over time instead of becoming an overwhelming backlog.

---

## Full Ownership

Manual Collections belong entirely to you.

Nothing depends on an external server.

Nothing disappears because a website changes.

Everything is stored inside your own workspace.

Your knowledge remains portable, searchable, and completely under your control.

---

## Collection Settings

Every Manual Collection provides its own configuration.

You can decide:

• Which Domain it belongs to

• Which Block it belongs to

• Daily scheduling limits

• Item type

This allows every collection to behave independently.

For example:

Algorithms

3 Questions per day

Linux

1 Article per day

Probability

5 Questions per day

Every collection follows its own rhythm.

---

## The Knowledge Pipeline

Manual Collections are the beginning of Hermes' knowledge pipeline.

Collect once.

Import once.

Learn gradually.

Instead of asking yourself every morning:

"What should I study today?"

Hermes quietly answers that question for you.

The system transforms your long-term curiosity into consistent daily learning without requiring constant planning.

---

## Why This Workflow?

Most applications help you collect information.

Very few help you consume it intentionally.

Manual Collections exist because learning should be deliberate, sustainable, and completely owned by you.

Rather than chasing today's recommendations, you build your own library and allow Hermes to guide you through it one thoughtful step at a time.

Knowledge enters because you invited it.

Never because an algorithm decided for you.

title: Import Collections
desc:
## Purpose

Import Collections allow you to bring large amounts of knowledge into Hermes within seconds.

Instead of manually creating hundreds of Questions or Articles one at a time, you can prepare a structured JSON file and import everything at once.

Hermes validates the file, stores every item locally, and immediately makes it available to the scheduling engine.

Importing knowledge should be a one-time task.

Learning from that knowledge should happen gradually over weeks or months.

---

## Why Import Exists

Building a personal knowledge system should never require repetitive work.

Imagine creating:

- 1,000 Probability Questions
- 500 Linux Articles
- 300 PyTorch Exercises
- 200 Research Papers

Creating each item manually would take hours.

Importing allows you to prepare the knowledge once and let Hermes organize the rest.

This keeps your focus on learning rather than data entry.

---

## Preparing Your Collection

Hermes accepts structured JSON files.

Each file contains a collection of either Questions or Articles.

Before importing, simply prepare the file using your preferred editor or generate it using AI.

Once ready, Hermes validates every item before saving anything into the database.

If the file contains errors, Hermes will explain the problem instead of importing incomplete or invalid data.

---

## Import Workflow

Importing follows a simple process.

Choose Collection

↓

Select JSON File

↓

Validate Structure

↓

Preview Import

↓

Import into Local Database

↓

Ready for Scheduling

Nothing is immediately added to Today's Pursuit.

Importing only stores knowledge.

Scheduling happens separately according to your Daily Limits.

---

## Local First

Every imported item is stored locally.

Your Questions and Articles remain available even without an internet connection.

Hermes never depends on an external server to preserve your knowledge.

Once imported, your collection belongs entirely to you.

---

## Safe Validation

Before importing, Hermes performs validation to ensure the file follows the expected structure.

Validation checks:

- Required fields
- Item type
- Collection format
- Missing values
- Invalid JSON

This prevents broken imports and keeps your knowledge base consistent.

---

## Growing Over Time

Importing is not something you'll do every day.

Instead, you'll gradually build larger collections over months.

Perhaps today you import:

- Probability Questions

Next week:

- Linux Articles

Later:

- Research Papers

Your library continues growing while Hermes quietly schedules everything in the background.

---

## Philosophy

Importing knowledge should feel effortless.

The effort should go into understanding the knowledge, not typing it into the application.

Hermes removes repetitive work so your attention stays where it belongs:

Learning.

title: Control Center
desc:
## Purpose

The Control Center is where you manage Hermes itself.

While the rest of Hermes is dedicated to learning, the Control Center is dedicated to maintaining your knowledge system.

Everything that affects your workspace, collections, imports, exports, settings, and organization begins here.

Think of it as the operating room of your personal knowledge system.

---

## Why A Control Center Exists

As your knowledge grows, managing it becomes just as important as collecting it.

Over time you may create:

- Multiple Workspaces
- Hundreds of Blocks
- Thousands of Questions
- Thousands of Articles
- Numerous Imports

Rather than scattering management features throughout the application, Hermes brings them together into one dedicated location.

Everything administrative belongs here.

Everything related to learning remains outside.

This separation keeps the rest of Hermes clean and distraction free.

---

## Workspace Management

The Control Center allows you to manage every Workspace stored inside Hermes.

From here you can:

- Create new Workspaces
- Switch between existing Workspaces
- Remove unused Workspaces
- Lock private Workspaces
- Configure Workspace settings

Every Workspace remains completely independent.

Changing one Workspace never affects another.

---

## Knowledge Pipeline

The Control Center is also home to the Knowledge Pipeline.

From here you can:

- Create Manual Collections
- Import JSON collections
- Import single articles
- Configure Import Settings
- Read the Collection Guide

Everything related to bringing knowledge into Hermes begins here.

---

## Export & Ownership

Your knowledge belongs to you.

The Control Center provides quick access to exporting your Workspaces and Collections.

Whether you want to create a backup, migrate to another device, or share knowledge with someone else, everything starts here.

Ownership should always remain one click away.

---

## Archive

Not every piece of knowledge needs to remain active forever.

Archived Items remain safely preserved without cluttering your daily workspace.

Nothing is permanently lost.

Whenever you need something again, simply restore it from the Archive.

Hermes prefers preservation over deletion.

---

## Guides

Hermes intentionally includes built-in guides.

These explain:

- How Manual Collections work
- Import formats
- Knowledge Pipelines
- Daily Scheduling
- JSON structures

Rather than sending users to external documentation, Hermes teaches itself from within.

Learning the application should feel as natural as using it.

---

## Settings

Application-wide preferences are also managed from the Control Center.

Examples include:

- Appearance
- Reading preferences
- Workspace behavior
- Visibility options
- Future customization features

These settings affect Hermes itself rather than individual Items.

---

## Designed To Stay Out Of Your Way

Most days you won't spend much time inside the Control Center.

That is intentional.

Its purpose is to organize your system so that the rest of Hermes can remain calm, focused, and free from unnecessary complexity.

You configure.

Hermes quietly does the rest.

---

## Philosophy

The Control Center exists so that management never interrupts learning.

Administration should happen in one place.

Understanding should happen everywhere else.

Hermes separates these responsibilities intentionally, allowing your attention to remain focused on knowledge rather than configuration.

A well-organized system should feel almost invisible.

The Control Center exists to keep it that way.

title: About Hermes
desc:
## Why Hermes Exists

Hermes was never created to become another productivity application.

It was created to solve a problem that became increasingly obvious over time.

Information had become effortless to collect.

Understanding had become increasingly difficult to preserve.

Articles were saved and forgotten.

Bookmarks accumulated faster than they were read.

Notes became scattered across multiple applications.

Ideas disappeared because they were never revisited.

Questions were answered once and never seen again.

The problem was never access to information.

The problem was building a system that could slowly transform information into understanding.

Hermes exists to solve that problem.

---

## A Different Philosophy

Most applications focus on helping you collect more.

Hermes focuses on helping you understand more.

Instead of endless feeds, Hermes gives you Today's Pursuit.

Instead of streaks, Hermes gives you Veritas.

Instead of counting completed tasks, Hermes preserves Evolutios.

Instead of storing everything as notes, Hermes recognizes that Questions, Articles, Ideas, Observations, Reflections, and Notes all represent different stages of learning.

Every feature inside Hermes exists because it supports understanding.

Nothing exists simply because it looks productive.

---

## Local First

Your knowledge belongs to you.

Hermes was designed around a simple principle:

If you spent years building your understanding, you should never lose ownership of it.

Your Workspaces live on your device.

Your Notes remain yours.

Your Questions remain yours.

Your Ideas remain yours.

No subscriptions are required to access your own thinking.

No cloud service is required to preserve it.

Hermes serves your knowledge.

Your knowledge should never serve Hermes.

---

## Learn Slowly

Modern software encourages constant consumption.

More videos.

More articles.

More notifications.

More recommendations.

Hermes intentionally slows everything down.

Rather than overwhelming you with hundreds of items, Hermes gradually surfaces carefully scheduled knowledge through Today's Pursuit.

Learning should feel sustainable.

Not exhausting.

Small progress repeated consistently becomes extraordinary over time.

---

## Build Your Own Library

Hermes does not decide what you should learn.

You do.

Import your own Questions.

Save your own Articles.

Write your own Notes.

Record your own Observations.

Develop your own Ideas.

Reflect in your own words.

Hermes simply provides the environment where that knowledge can continue growing for years.

Your library reflects your curiosity, not someone else's algorithm.

---

## Growth Over Productivity

Finishing work is important.

Changing the way you think is even more important.

Hermes measures growth through understanding rather than activity.

A single Evolutio may matter more than dozens of completed tasks.

A thoughtful Reflection may outlast an entire week's productivity.

The goal has never been to do more.

The goal has always been to understand more deeply.

---

## Built To Last

Hermes was designed with a long-term perspective.

Knowledge collected today should remain useful years from now.

Ideas should be revisited.

Questions should continue teaching.

Articles should remain searchable.

Reflections should reveal your growth.

The application should become more valuable with time, not less.

Every year you spend using Hermes should make the next year even richer.

---

## An Invitation

Hermes is not a finished destination.

It is a companion for continuous learning.

Whether you're studying engineering, building a startup, researching new ideas, or simply trying to understand the world a little better, Hermes grows alongside you.

The knowledge inside Hermes is not a database.

It is a reflection of your curiosity.

And over time, it becomes a reflection of who you are becoming.

---

## Final Thought

Information is easy to collect.

Understanding is difficult to preserve.

Hermes exists to bridge the distance between the two.
